description: Music discovery — from lo-fi hip hop to jazz

turns.content: I've been listening to a lot of lo-fi hip hop lately. Any other genres I might like?

Lo-fi hip hop lovers usually fall into two camps: people who like it for the mood and texture (mellow, late-night feel), or people who like the hip hop production elements specifically. Which resonates more for you? Either way, most people tend to also enjoy: jazz (especially 1960s Blue Note records — Miles Davis Kind of Blue is the gateway), chillhop (basically lo-fi with more jazz influence), and bedroom pop (Rex Orange County, Clairo). If you like the meditative repetitive quality, ambient music — Brian Eno's Ambient 1 — could work really well.
Definitely the mood and texture side. I put it on when I'm studying or coding.

For studying and focus you would probably also love: Nujabes (Japanese producer who was a huge influence on lo-fi hip hop — his Metaphorical Music album is essential), Tycho (instrumental electronic with a warm analog feel), and Four Tet (more complex but similarly textured). For something slightly different with the same 'brain goes into flow state' quality: Jon Hopkins, Nils Frahm (piano-based electronic), or Max Richter. Max Richter's Sleep album is 8 hours designed for sleeping but works perfectly for deep focus work.
Nujabes sounds exactly right. Also I've never explored jazz — where do I actually start without it feeling overwhelming?

Miles Davis Kind of Blue — that is the starting point, non-negotiable. It is the best-selling jazz album ever for a reason: accessible without being simplified, beautiful without being background music. After that: John Coltrane A Love Supreme for the spiritual meditative side. Bill Evans Waltz for Debby for intimate late-night piano jazz. The beautiful thing is jazz and lo-fi hip hop have a direct lineage — Nujabes sampled jazz constantly. You are not moving to a foreign genre, you are going back to the source material. Try Kind of Blue this week; you will probably recognise textures you have already been loving.
